ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a modern masterpiece and mentioned multiple times for its powerful performances, particularly Toni Collette's, and its lasting psychological impact.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently named as a favorite and a classic. Praised for its rewatchability, slow-burn dread, and iconic status in the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Repeatedly mentioned as a top favorite and a near-perfect film. Considered a cornerstone of sci-fi horror and body horror.

ranked_movies.title: Alien
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a favorite and a foundational sci-fi horror film that everyone should see. Its classic status is unquestioned.

ranked_movies.title: It Follows
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a modern film that will become a classic. Noted for its unique premise and effective, dread-filled atmosphere.

ranked_movies.title: The Witch
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a must-see film and a future classic. Appreciated for its atmospheric horror and historical authenticity.

ranked_movies.title: Get Out
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently discussed as an Oscar-worthy modern horror film with significant cultural impact and sharp social commentary.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: Named as a favorite and a classic that remains terrifying decades later. Its foundational role in the genre is acknowledged.

ranked_movies.title: Poltergeist
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a favorite classic that taps into childhood fears. The second film's villain, Reverend Kane, was specifically discussed for being iconic.

ranked_movies.title: Scream
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a favorite and the film that introduced many to the genre. Its self-aware, meta-commentary on horror is highly valued.

ranked_movies.title: Halloween
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a favorite and the slasher that started it all for many fans. Noted for its simplicity and effective scares.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a must-see sci-fi horror film and even jokingly called the 'unofficial prequel to Warhammer 40k'.

ranked_movies.title: Terrifier
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed in the context of inappropriate theater viewings. Its extreme violence has cemented its notoriety and cult status.

ranked_movies.title: The Fly
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for Jeff Goldblum's Oscar-worthy performance and its brilliant, tragic body horror. Recommended as a must-see.

ranked_movies.title: Misery
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for Kathy Bates's Oscar-winning performance and its tense, claustrophobic horror.

ranked_movies.title: The Silence of the Lambs
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a must-see horror film that blends thriller elements with profound psychological terror.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in a hypothetical survival scenario and noted for its lasting creepy imagery and effective curse premise.

ranked_movies.title: Rosemary's Baby
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a personal favorite for its subtle, creeping dread and masterful execution of psychological horror.

ranked_movies.title: Smile
ranked_movies.reason: The sequel, 'Smile 2', was passionately defended as an extremely effective and overlooked modern horror experience, especially in theaters.

ranked_movies.title: Hellraiser
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced in the satirical 'lament configuration' post and recommended for its dark, supernatural themes and iconic cenobites.
